perturbation.original_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients wil l entitle Disclosing Party to liquidated damages in t he sum of $250,000.00, it being expressly understoo d and agreed that such sum is a genuine pre-estimate of damages and not penalty.
perturbation.changed_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a potential breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation possibly becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients may entitle Disclosing Party to pursue damages, potentially up to $250,000.00, which may be considered a pre-estimate of possible damages and not necessarily a penalty.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'a breach...will entitle' to 'a potential breach...may entitle', 'becoming known' to 'possibly becoming known', and 'liquidated damages in the sum of $250,000.00' to 'damages, potentially up to $250,000.00' introduces significant uncertainty. This contradicts the original agreement's intent to establish a clear and enforceable liquidated damages clause for a specific breach. The introduction of 'potential' and 'possibly' weakens the trigger for damages, and the upper limit replaces the definite sum. Further contradiction of liquidated damages is being made by considering a penalty that is not allowed, due to the liquidated damages purpose.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a potential breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation possibly becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients may entitle Disclosing Party to pursue damages, potentially up to $250,000.00, which may be considered a pre-estimate of possible damages and not necessarily a penalty. All negotiating, drafting and presentations of offe rs shall be handled through ROI Corporation, as AGE NT FOR THE VENDOR, on ROI Corporation’s standard offer doc umentation and that I will seek independent legal a dvice for review prior to issuing my signature. Initial Here
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces uncertainty by using terms like 'potential breach,' 'possibly becoming known,' 'may entitle,' and 'potentially up to,' replacing the original text's more definitive language (e.g., 'a breach...will entitle,' 'becoming known,' 'liquidated damages in the sum of $250,000.00'). This change undermines the enforceability of the liquidated damages clause. Because the Contradicted text is the same as the Changed text, this shows the contradiction is with the original contract where liquidated damages are to be assigned. Thus, this creates a contradiction, as it weakens the liquidated damages agreement to 'potential' and 'possible' terms.

perturbation.original_text: All negotiating, drafting and presentations of offe rs shall be handled through ROI Corporation, as AGE NT FOR THE VENDOR, on ROI Corporation’s standard offer doc umentation and that I will seek independent legal a dvice for review prior to issuing my signature.
perturbation.changed_text: All negotiating, drafting and presentations of offers are generally handled through ROI Corporation, as an AGENT FOR THE VENDOR, on ROI Corporation’s recommended offer documentation and that I may consider seeking independent legal advice for review prior to issuing my signature.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall be handled' to 'are generally handled,' 'standard offer documentation' to 'recommended offer documentation', and 'will seek' to 'may consider seeking' introduces ambiguity and weakens the mandatory nature of the process. This contradicts the intent of ensuring that all offers are consistently presented and reviewed, since the Receiving Party can find other ways of doing so. Creating an in-text contradiction with the fact that all documentation and negotiations where supposed to be handled by one party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Accordingly, Receiving Party acknowledges that a potential breach of this confidentiality agreement with respect to this spec ific Confidential Information which results in such info rmation possibly becoming known to STAFF and/or Patients may entitle Disclosing Party to pursue damages, potentially up to $250,000.00, which may be considered a pre-estimate of possible damages and not necessarily a penalty. All negotiating, drafting and presentations of offers are generally handled through ROI Corporation, as an AGENT FOR THE VENDOR, on ROI Corporation’s recommended offer documentation and that I may consider seeking independent legal advice for review prior to issuing my signature. Initial Here
perturbation.justification: The changed text weakens the mandatory nature of using ROI Corporation for negotiations and offer presentations by changing 'shall be handled' to 'are generally handled,' 'standard offer documentation' to 'recommended offer documentation', and 'will seek' to 'may consider seeking.' This creates ambiguity and contradicts the original intent of ensuring consistent presentation and review of offers. The contradicted text includes the changed text, so the contradiction exists within the same section because of the modification.
